assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.VIII p.73-75 "The Desire of Society" - "get at it."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Choose a habit you would like to cultivate and tell a little about why. Draw a chart for keeping up with your new habit and check in daily.

• COMPOSITION - Journal
Tell of a time when your desire for society was met. How did that experience impact/affect you?

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.VIII p.75-77 "Dangers attending" - "the great world."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log. Start a chart where you can list desirable human character traits on one side and their daemons on the other.

• COMPOSITION
Tell of a time when you took a "share in the interests of the great world" by listening with all your heart to someone who shared about a profession, skill, subject, or experience with which you were unfamiliar.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.VIII p.77-78 "The Desire of Knowledge" - "ourselves!"

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

• COMPOSITION - Compare/Contrast
Compare and contrast the desire for knowledge and curiosity.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.VIII p.78-79 "Emulation and the Love" - "or in life."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

• COMPOSITION - Cause & Effect
How is the love of knowledge smothered by emulation? Include "marks" and "places" in your examples.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.VIII p.79-80 "All Persons have Powers" - "the battle."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

• COMPOSITION
Tell of a time when you have thrown yourself into your work and it was a delight to you. OR Tell of a character in a book who has done so and the result was joy.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.I p.81-83 "The Lords of the House" - "feel it."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update character traits chart. Think of a new habit you would like to cultivate and create a tracking chart.

• COMPOSITION - Poetry
Write a poem about selfless love using your own examples patterned after soldiers, mothers, nurses, missionaries, etc.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.I p.83-86 "Love delights in the Goodness" - "Waiting."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

• COMPOSITION - Expository
Explain the four ways in which a person is able to recognize authentic love.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.II p.87-89 "Knights and Ladies of Pity" - "idle pity."

• How could you show that all people possess and express pity?

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.II p.89-90 "Self-Pity." - "sorry for ourselves."

• CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart this week.

• COMPOSITION - Story
Write a fable-type short story in which a character's self-pity is exposed and he overcomes it.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.III p.91-92 "Reform the World" - "better work."

• Why are faults not the whole of a person? Have you ever felt like all you could see was a person's faults? What did you do?

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.III p.92-94 "The Affairs of Goodwill." - "meaning for us."

• Can you give an example for each of the three enemies of Goodwill?

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Write a brief reflection on how you did with habit cultivation this term.

Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to the course. Questions will come from: Ourselves
assignment_teachersNote:
★ Ethics: Part 2 — TERM 1 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Ourselves: Book I: Name some enemies of goodwill and show how they behave.

assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ NOTE
Be sure to narrate in a variety of ways.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.IV p.95-96 "Sympathy with One, " - "touch of this key."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update character traits chart. Think of a new habit you would like to cultivate and create a tracking chart.

• COMPOSITION - Letter
Show sympathy to someone you know by sharing your interest in music, poetry, art, or anything that gives you joy.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.IV p.96-97 "A Lever to Raise." - "easily discerned."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log. Start a chart where you can list desirable human character traits on one side and their daemons on the other.

• ILLUSTRATE
Draw a scene in which a person shows Sympathy by empathizing with someone who is suffering.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.IV p.98 "Tact." - "Vanity, Dislike, Antipathy."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

• COMPOSITION - Expository
How is egotism a foe of Sympathy? Include examples.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.V p.99-100 "That best portion" - "who come in their way."

• For your narration, illustrate and caption three examples of the kindness of courtesy.

• CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart this week.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ & NARRATE
Ch.V p.100-101
"Simplicity." - "-- Wordsworth."

➜ DISCUSS
• Can there be kindness without simplicity?

• CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.V p.101-102
"Kindness in Construction." - "-- Herbert."

• What are the effects of a kind construction and those of an ungentle construction.? Give examples.

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update character traits chart. Think of a new habit you would like to cultivate and create a tracking chart.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ & NARRATE
Ch.VI p.103-104
"Generous Impulses" - "the generous man."

➜ DISCUSS
What are some generous movements in history?

• CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

• COMPOSITION - Timeline
Make a timeline of 6-8 generous movements in history.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.VI p.105 "Generosity is Costly," - "his own family."

• Explain how generosity is both costly and profitable.

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.VI p.106-107 "Fallacious Notions" - "-- Lowell."

• CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart this week.

• COMPOSITION - Outline
Outline the three false notions that prevent generosity.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ & NARRATE
Ch.VII p.108-109 "The Gladness of a Grateful" - "value to us."

➜ DISCUSS
Do you have a relative or friend from whom you have received double joys?

• CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart this week.

• COMPOSITION - Letter
Write a letter to a relative or friend from whom you have received double joys. Acknowledge and thank them for at least three kindnesses.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.VII p.110-111 "The Reproach of Ingratitude" - "thankful!"

• Describe the sin of ingratitude. What examples from your books can you think of that illustrate this sin?

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Write a brief reflection on how you did with habit cultivation this term.

Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to the course. Questions will come from: Ourselves
assignment_teachersNote:
★ Ethics: Part 2 — TERM 2 EXAM QUESTIONS
· Ourselves: Book I: Name some false ideas that hold back generosity OR Show how egotism destroys sympathy.

assignment_description:
⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ NOTE
Be sure to narrate in a variety of ways.

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.VIII p.112-115
"We all have Courage." - "the unbelieving."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update character traits chart. Think of a new habit you would like to cultivate and create a tracking chart.

• COMPOSITION
Tell how we are able to "take the present without any fearful looking forward." Provide three examples.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.VIII p.115-116 "The Courage of our Opinions." - "elders."

• Give your opinion on any topic of interest. Do you think your opinion is based on knowledge and principle which you possess, or is it based on what everybody says?

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log. Start a chart where you can list desirable human character traits on one side and their daemons on the other.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.VIII p.116-117 "The Courage" - "faithful souls."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

• COMPOSITION - Persuasive
Show that intellectual panic is responsible for many failures. Give three examples.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.IX p.118-120 "Loyalty of Youth." - "a cause of joy."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

• COMPOSITION - Debate
Are we free to choose our loyalties?

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ & NARRATE
Ch.IX p.120-122 "Loyalty to Country." - "on every hand."

• To what cause of your local government are you interested in giving your time, labor, and means to advance it? What can you do to participate and work towards advancing this cause?

➜ DISCUSS
To what cause of your local government can you give of your time, labor, and means to advance it?

• CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.IX p.122-124 "Loyalty to Personal Ties." - "far more."

• "Perhaps there is always more of the best to be had in sticking to that we have got than in looking out continually for a new shop for every sort of ware." Explain this quote. How does this principle of loyalty apply to your life, someone you know, and/or someone about whom you have read?

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update character traits chart. Think of a new habit you would like to cultivate and create a tracking chart.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.IX p.124-125 "Thoroughness." - "alien to flight."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

• COMPOSITION - Cause & Effect
Write about three causes of disloyalty (what loyalty forbids) and their effects. Give an example of each.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ & NARRATE
Ch.X p.126-128 "Pride of Life." - "ourselves for the fact."

• "What we call Humility is often a form of Hypocrisy." Explain this quote. Can you think of three examples of your own which illustrate this idea?

➜ DISCUSS
What does Mason mean when she says, "What we call Humility is often a form of Hypocrisy"?

• CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.X p.128-130 "Humility one with Simplicity." - "-- Trench."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

• COMPOSITION - Point of View
Imagine you are a parent who is trying to help your teen to recognize and to overcome Pride. What advice would you give your teen? Name 10 actions you would recommend to your teen to get away from the thought of him or herself.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.XI p.131-133 "It is a seemly fashion" - "have given away."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Update habits log and character traits chart.

• COMPOSITION - Script
Write a few scenes in which you show through the characters that gladness is catching.

⍞ Materials: Ourselves: Book 1

➜ READ, NARRATE, & DISCUSS
Ch.XI p.133-135 "Gladness is Perennial." - "I say, rejoice."

➜ CITIZENSHIP NOTEBOOK
Write a brief reflection on how you did with habit cultivation this term.

• EXTRA HELPINGS
Act out a scene or two in which someone is at a delightful event, but he or she is sullen and unhappy. What was the cause of the person's moodiness? How did it affect his or her time at the event among the other guests? How did it affect other guests?

Exam Week

➜ EXAMS
Answer question(s) related to the course. Questions will come from: Ourselves
